stacy was amazing! she was by far the best and most helpful and articulate xfinity/comcast customer service person i’ve worked with in more than 20 years of having comcast. she was kind, helpful, intelligent and funny and helped me to find a more appropriate plan to suit my needs in my upcoming move. she spent time researching what would work best for me and helped me to get exactly what needed was looking for. she had a great personality and was the kind of person i would enjoy as a friend. she took the time to be a true problem solver, which i was so appreciative for. please give this woman a raise, but keep her in a customer-facing role—she is fantastic and truly worth promoting so she can train others to be as good as she is. i had been so annoyed with your app and ai chatbot hanging up on me (twice), that finally, reaching stacy was a huge relief. when a person is stressed in the middle of a move, one does not want ai non sentient beings wasting one’s time. stacy’s the kind of person who can restore a person’s faith in corporate america! brava!  she did mine, and impressed me that comcast has an actual native english speaker working to help us. thank you!

( i should not of cursed, but it’s not like i cursed at her, but that was still my bad). her telling me that just enraged me even more. she transferred me, but it went to the billing department automation machine, so that elevated my anger even more. i was finally able to get somebody at the escalation department. and i told him that i was pissed because the other two agents not today but previously never told me there would be an equipment fee if i didn’t turn it in. i had moved from my house and the equipment was in a box. i didn’t want to dig through all my stuff to find the box and they said it was ok. they never told me i would be charged for the equipment by two different agents.

5 is a neutral number in a 0-10 scale. i chose neutral because negotiating through the labyrinthine system to get to the right person for information. no one likes to go through it but all big businesses have it. nothing about trying to get to an answer is easy and thus i cannot recommend it to anyonebutit is no worse for xfinity than it is for any gargantuan business. i need 1 specific answer and then a good guess about the handling of a second payment. i eventually got both pieces of information but not without spending excessive time doing so. 5 represents the fact that the task was accomplished but not without unnecessary effort. for example, every time i call for our account i am required to get permission from my wife to talk about our account and many times i ask to be added as a secondary contact. this results in being required to get my wife’s permission to speak about our account during the next call because adding me as a secondary contact does not save from one call to another. as long as i keep my expectations at a level commensurate with how big businesses operate, i can get to my information. 5 reflects that the system works but only if i know how to work within it. no harmno foul.

my internet has been intermittent at best for 2 weeks now. 3 field techs have come to inspect and leave saying everything is fine. the issue remains unresolved. i've spent hundreds of hours of dollars trying to troubleshoot on my own by replacing modems, coax, connectors, and ground blocks because i have to wait 2 days between field tech arrivals. i'm confident the 4th tech, arriving tomorrow, will not resolve the issue because no one is looking beyond the tap, amplifier, or facility. what is causing frequent t3/t4 in my modem logs?! each blame the previous tech for missing something and do nothing more than reboot the modem and tighten coax connectors after they disconnect to inspect.each call i need to explain the issue over and over again from the beginning. i would like to talk to an expert. i called and asked to talk to a supervisor, too busy, would call back in 2 hours. no call.perhaps just as annoying as having internet drops every 20 minutes is when they ask me if i want to purchase a phone line. please no, you can't even get my internet working.at this point i cannot and will not recommend xfinity. i'm heading toward 10% downtime. the internet is unreliable as my confidence in xfinity, up and down.
